steps.title: Determine if the function is increasing or decreasing
steps.content: Scan the graph from left to right. The $y$-values rise as $x$ increases, so the function is increasing. That eliminates choice B, decreasing linear, and choice D, decreasing exponential.

steps.title: Determine if the curve is linear or exponential
steps.content: A linear function would be a straight line with constant slope. This graph is not straight: it is nearly flat on the left, passes near $(0,1)$, and then becomes much steeper to the right. That increasing steepness is the visual signature of exponential growth.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The graph is increasing and exponential, not increasing linear. Choice C is correct.
